dangerous situations. Such a support is an unshakable faith in
the protective power and wisdom of God. A man of faith is
guided in his life by the precepts of Sastras as well as by Ihe
practices of great men of yore who have bequeathed to us Vedas
and Vedangas, which include the science of Jyautisa, Astronomy
and Astrology. Let me digress here a little: The other day a
certain gentleman railing against astrology remarked that while
Astronomy was a science Astrology was not. Such people forget
that the basis of astrology is nothing but the astronomical science.
If the cause is scientific, the effect also should be scientific. Now
coming to the subject of Paiicanga, we can safely declare that no
man can live and act in society effectively unless hc,is conversant
with the parts of Calendar. In Indian parlance especially in
religious matters this calendar takes the form of the Almanac
which shows many other things apart from the above-named five
elements.
